commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e05fa8a1104f80e9a81b44671337d4c351f2cb59)
commands.review_summary:
The PR maps `cacheRetention: "standard"` to `"short"` for Anthropic-family cache retention resolution and adds positive and negative resolver regression tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +95. Total +101 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main gates explicit Bedrock Claude cache config into the Anthropic Bedrock family, then accepts only `none`, `short`, and `long`, so `standard` resolves to `undefined` before provider dispatch. I did not run a live Bedrock request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer config-contract decision, not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(agents): map cacheRetention 'standard' to 'short' for Bedrock Claude models
target.body:
## Root Cause

`resolveCacheRetention()` in `prompt-cache-retention.ts` only recognizes `'none'`, `'short'`, and `'long'` via strict equality. When a user sets `cacheRetention: 'standard'` in their model params (a valid Bedrock cache retention value), the value falls through the check and returns `undefined` instead of resolving to `'short'`. The Bedrock provider's own default masks this by falling back to `'short'` when `cacheRetention` is `undefined`, making the bug silent — the user's explicit `'standard'` setting is effectively ignored without any warning.

## Summary

- Bug: `cacheRetention='standard'` (a valid Bedrock cache retention level) is silently ignored for Bedrock Claude models because `resolveCacheRetention()` doesn't recognize the value. The provider's fallback masks the issue, so users never see an error.
- Fix: Added a mapping of `'standard'` → `'short'` in `resolveCacheRetention()`, scoped to the `family` (Anthropic/Bedrock cache family) gate so Google and prompt-cache-key providers are not affected. Added regression tests verifying the alias does not leak outside its intended scope.
- Fixes #94482

## Revisions

This revision addresses ClawSweeper review feedback:
- [P0] Added stronger real behavior proof: resolved `cacheRetention: "standard"` against the built binary's `resolveCacheRetention()` at `dist/extra-params-BffSE4-w.js` — confirming the code path active at runtime.
- [P2] Scoped `standard` → `short` alias to `family` (Anthropic/Bedrock cache family) only — Google and prompt-cache-key providers no longer accept the alias.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts` — 15/15 passed (7 new tests + 8 existing, including 2 regression tests for Google/prompt-cache-key)
- `pnpm build` — exit code 0, verified dist contains the fix
- Binary commit matches HEAD: OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (e05fa8a) == git rev-parse HEAD e05fa8a
- Import cycles: `pnpm check:import-cycles` — clean
- No lock files changed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: User's `cacheRetention='standard'` config in model params was silently ignored for Bedrock Claude models. This fix maps it to `'short'` only within the Anthropic/Bedrock cache family, and adds regression tests that Google and prompt-cache-key providers do not accept the alias.

Real environment tested: Linux (4.19.112), Node v24.13.1, branch `fix/issue-94482-bedrock-cacheretention-standard` at commit `e05fa8a110`. Binary `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (e05fa8a)` confirmed matching HEAD.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm build
node -e "
import('./dist/extra-params-BffSE4-w.js').then(m => {
  const fn = m.s;  // resolveCacheRetention in bundled dist
  const tests = [
    ['Bedrock Claude Sonnet 4.6',       { cacheRetention: 'standard' }, 'amazon-bedrock', 'openai-completions', 'us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6'],
    ['Bedrock Claude Opus 4.6',         { cacheRetention: 'standard' }, 'amazon-bedrock', 'openai-completions', 'anthropic.claude-opus-4-6'],
    ['Direct Anthropic Claude',         { cacheRetention: 'standard' }, 'anthropic', 'anthropic-messages', 'claude-sonnet-4-6'],
    ['Vertex Anthropic Claude',         { cacheRetention: 'standard' }, 'anthropic-vertex', 'anthropic-messages', 'claude-sonnet-4-6'],
    ['Google Gemini (expect undefined)', { cacheRetention: 'standard' }, 'google', 'google-generative-ai', 'gemini-3.1-pro-preview'],
    ['Bedrock Nova (expect undefined)',  { cacheRetention: 'standard' }, 'amazon-bedrock', 'openai-completions', 'amazon.nova-micro-v1:0'],
    ['OpenAI GPT-4 (expect undefined)',  { cacheRetention: 'standard' }, 'openai', undefined, 'gpt-4'],
  ];
  for (const [label, ...args] of tests) {
    const result = fn(...args);
    console.log(label + ' -> ' + (result === undefined ? 'undefined' : JSON.stringify(result)));
  }
});
"
```

Evidence after fix: Runtime output from the built binary exercising `cacheRetention: "standard"` against `dist/extra-params-BffSE4-w.js`:
```
Bedrock Claude Sonnet 4.6 -> "short"
Bedrock Claude Opus 4.6 -> "short"
Direct Anthropic Claude -> "short"
Vertex Anthropic Claude -> "short"
Google Gemini (expect undefined) -> undefined
Bedrock Nova (expect undefined) -> undefined
OpenAI GPT-4 (expect undefined) -> undefined
```

Observed result after fix: `cacheRetention='standard'` correctly maps to `'short'` for all eligible Anthropic providers (Bedrock Claude, direct Anthropic, Anthropic Vertex). Google and prompt-cache-key providers correctly return `undefined` for the `'standard'` alias, confirming it does not leak beyond the intended Anthropic/Bedrock cache family.

What was not tested: Live E2E against a real Bedrock endpoint (requires AWS credentials). The fix is a purely additive string-mapping change in cache retention resolution logic with an additional scope gate, comprehensively covered by unit tests including explicit regression tests for non-target providers, and verified against the built binary's runtime behavior.
target.author: xialonglee
